target.name: Astera Labs
target.founder: Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, Casey Morrison

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 23.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Cerebras
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $23B

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Astera Labs is a publicly traded semiconductor company founded by Jitendra Mohan, Sanjay Gajendra, and Casey Morrison, having raised $713M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 4 same-sector, same-stage public companies in Chips & Semiconductors: Cerebras ($23.0B), Credo Semiconductor ($5.0B), Ouster ($1.5B), and Aeva Technologies ($1.0B).

The peer-median method represents the sole applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across these four comparables is $3.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B at the low end to $23.0B at the high end. A public-comp anchoring methodology using sector price-to-sales multiples (median 23.4x across ALAB, NVDA, and AMD) cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue figures, typical for private-stage companies. This constraint limits the analysis to peer positioning alone.

The triangulated valuation range is $2.8B (low) to $3.7B (high), centered on $3.2B. This estimate reflects the median of available public-stage semiconductor comparables but carries material dispersion risk, evidenced by the $22B spread between minimum and maximum peer valuations. Key uncertainties include actual revenue run-rate, gross margins relative to peers, and market-share trajectory in Astera's specific subsegments. The single-method basis warrants caution; additional revenue disclosure or margin analysis would strengthen conviction.
